---
applyTo: '**/*.ts,**/*.tsx'
---

# Project coding standards for TypeScript and React

Apply the [general coding guidelines](./general-coding.instructions.md) to all code.

## TypeScript Guidelines

- Use TypeScript for all new code
- Follow functional programming principles where possible
- Use interfaces for data structures and type definitions
- Prefer immutable data (const, readonly)
- Use optional chaining (?.) and nullish coalescing (??) operators
- Use generics for type safety
- Use type assertions for type narrowing
- Use type guards for runtime type checking
- Use discriminated unions for exhaustiveness checking
- Use enums for strongly typed constants
- Use type aliases for complex types
- Use utility types for type manipulation
- Use mapped types for type transformation
- Use conditional types for type inference
- Use keyof types for indexing
- Use index signatures for dynamic properties
- Use type inference for variables and parameters
- Use type inference for return types
- Use type inference for function parameters
- Use type inference for function return types
- Use type inference for object properties
- Use type inference for array elements
- Use type inference for tuple elements
- Use type inference for function arguments
- Use type inference for function return types
- Use type inference for object properties
- Use type inference for array elements
- Use type inference for tuple elements

## React Guidelines

- Use functional components with hooks
- Follow the React hooks rules (no conditional hooks)
- Use React.FC type for components with children
- Keep components small and focused
- Use CSS modules for component styling
- Use TypeScript with React.FC for components with children
- Use React.memo for memoization
- Use React.useCallback for memoized callbacks
- Use React.useMemo for memoized values
- Use React.useRef for DOM references
- Use React.useImperativeHandle for custom hooks
- Use React.useLayoutEffect for layout effects
- Use React.useDebugValue for debug values
- Use React.useTransition for transitions
- Use React.useDeferredValue for deferred values
- Use React.useInsertionEffect for insertion effects
- Use React.useSyncExternalStore for sync external stores
- Use React.useId for unique IDs
- Use React.useTransition for transitions
- Use React.useDeferredValue for deferred values
- Use React.useInsertionEffect for insertion effects
- Use React.useSyncExternalStore for sync external stores
